segments.text: Again Jesus began to teach by the lake. The crowd that gathered around him was so large that he got into a boat and sat in it out on the lake, while all the people were along the shore at the water's edge.
He taught them many things by parables, and in his teaching said:
Listen! A farmer went out to sow his seed.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path, and the birds came and ate it up.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Some fell on rocky places, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly, because the soil was shallow.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the plants, so that they did not bear grain.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Still other seed fell on good soil. It came up, grew and produced a crop, some multiplying thirty, some sixty, some a hundred times.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus said,
Whoever has ears to hear, let them hear.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When he was alone, the Twelve and the others around him asked him about the parables.
He told them,
The secret of the kingdom of God has been given to you. But to those on the outside everything is said in parables
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: so that, 'they may be ever seeing but never perceiving, and ever hearing but never understanding; otherwise they might turn and be forgiven!'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus said to them,
Don't you understand this parable? How then will you understand any parable?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The farmer sows the word.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Some people are like seed along the path, where the word is sown. As soon as they hear it, Satan comes and takes away the word that was sown in them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Others, like seed sown on rocky places, hear the word and at once receive it with joy.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But since they have no root, they last only a short time. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, they quickly fall away.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Still others, like seed sown among thorns, hear the word;
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: but the worries of this life, the deceitfulness of wealth and the desires for other things come in and choke the word, making it unfruitful.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Others, like seed sown on good soil, hear the word, accept it, and produce a crop—some thirty, some sixty, some a hundred times what was sown.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said to them,
Do you bring in a lamp to put it under a bowl or a bed? Instead, don't you put it on its stand?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For whatever is hidden is meant to be disclosed, and whatever is concealed is meant to be brought out into the open.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If anyone has ears to hear, let them hear.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He continued,
Consider carefully what you hear. With the measure you use, it will be measured to you—and even more.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever has will be given more; whoever does not have, even what they have will be taken from them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: The Parable of the Growing Seed

segments.text: He also said,
This is what the kingdom of God is like. A man scatters seed on the ground.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Night and day, whether he sleeps or gets up, the seed sprouts and grows, though he does not know how.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All by itself the soil produces grain—first the stalk, then the head, then the full kernel in the head.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As soon as the grain is ripe, he puts the sickle to it, because the harvest has come.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: The Parable of the Mustard Seed

segments.text: Again he said,
What shall we say the kingdom of God is like, or what parable shall we use to describe it?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: It is like a mustard seed, which is the smallest of all seeds on earth.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Yet when planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all garden plants, with such big branches that the birds can perch in its shade.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: With many similar parables Jesus spoke the word to them, as much as they could understand.
He did not say anything to them without using a parable. But when he was alone with his own disciples, he explained everything.
That day when evening came, he said to his disciples,
Let us go over to the other side.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Leaving the crowd behind, they took him along, just as he was, in the boat. There were also other boats with him.
A furious squall came up, and the waves broke over the boat, so that it was nearly swamped.
Jesus was in the stern, sleeping on a cushion. The disciples woke him and said to him,
Teacher, don't you care if we drown?
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: He got up, rebuked the wind and said to the waves,
Quiet! Be still!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said to his disciples,
Why are you so afraid? Do you still have no faith?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They were terrified and asked each other,
Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!
segments.speaker: Disciples
